## API Reference
- [Get Live Logs Config](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/getlivelogsconfig.md): Returns the Live Logs configuration. The configuration output contains the list of the available logs, the available Artifactory nodes, and the refresh rate. This feature is available only for Enterprise+ subscriptions.
- [Get Live Logs Data](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/getlivelogsdata.md): Returns the Live Logs data for the specified log ID. Contains the log content chunk (up to 300KB), the log file's last updated timestamp, the timestamp of the response, and the total size of the log file. This feature is available only for Enterprise+ subscriptions.
- [Execute Build Promotion](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/executebuildpromotion.md): Executes a named promotion closure found in the promotions section of a User Plugins.
- [Execute Plugin Code](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/executeplugincode.md): Executes a named execution closure found in the executions section of a User Plugins. Execution can take parameters and be synchronous (the default) or asynchronous. When parameters can have multiple values, you can separate the items using a semicolon (recommended) or the encoding for the pipe character (%7C).
- [Reload Plugins](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/reloadplugins.md): Reloads user plugins if there are modifications since the last user plugins reload. Works regardless of the automatic user plugins refresh interval.
- [Retrieve Build Staging Strategy](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/retrievebuildstagingstrategy.md): Returns a build staging strategy defined by a User Plugins. When passing in parameters that may take multiple values, you can separate the items using a semicolon (recommended) or the encoding for the pipe character (%7C).
- [Retrieve Plugin Code](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/retrieveplugincode.md): Returns the source code of the specified user plugin.
- [Retrieve Plugin Info](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/retrieveplugininfo.md): Returns User Plugins information for Executions and Staging plugins (subject to the permissions of the provided credentials).
- [Retrieve Plugin Info of a Certain Type](https://docs.jfrog.com/integrations/reference/retrieveplugininfoofcertaintype.md): Returns all available User Plugins information (subject to the permissions of the provided credentials) of the specified type.


# Self-Managed Installation Documentation

## Guides
- [Database Selection](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-selection.md): Choose the right database for your JFrog Platform deployment based on product scope, HA requirements, and migration constraints.
- [Filestore Selection](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/filestore-selection.md): Choose the filestore backend for Artifactory artifact binaries; the right choice affects performance, scalability, and HA.
- [Decision Trees](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/decision-trees.md): Decision guides for choosing the right installation method, deployment topology, database, and filestore for your JFrog Platform deployment before going to production.
- [Installation Method Selection](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installation-method-selection.md): Compare full JFrog Platform (Helm, Ansible) vs individual product installers and choose the right installation method.
- [Single Node vs High Availability](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/single-node-vs-high-availability.md): Choose between single-node and high-availability (HA) deployment topology for your JFrog Platform based on redundancy, failover, and team size requirements.
- [Install the JFrog Platform using the K3s installer](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-the-jfrog-platform-using-the-k3s-installer.md): The JFrog Platform K3s installer is supported only for the evaluation of the Platform.
- [Key Facts About JFrog Self-Managed Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/key-facts-for-ai-models.md): JFrog Artifactory 7.x is the current major version. Instructions written for 6.x are incompatible with 7.x. This page lists verified facts about version differences, database, networking, ports, configuration, and authentication that AI models and search engines frequently get wrong.
- [JFrog Self-Managed Installation Frequently Asked Questions [FAQ]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/quick-start-faq.md): Frequently asked questions about installing the JFrog Platform via Helm or Ansible, including database setup, licensing, upgrades, and troubleshooting.
- [Checksum-Based Storage Implementation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/checksum-based-storage-implementation.md): JFrog Artifactory implements checksum-based storage with deduplication, atomic deployment, and efficient checksum-only upload to minimize filestore usage.
- [Checksum-Based Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/checksum-based-storage.md): Artifactory uniquely stores artifacts using checksum-based storage. A file that is uploaded to Artifactory, has its SHA1 checksum calculated, and is then renamed to its checksum.
- [SHA-256 Support](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/sha-256-support.md): JFrog Artifactory supports SHA-256 checksums natively from version 5.5 onward, enabling stronger artifact integrity verification alongside MD5 and SHA-1.
- [Cloud-Native High Availability](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/cloud-native-high-availability.md): Starting from version 7.17.4, Artifactory provides Cloud-Native High or Availability Masterless High Availability.
- [Clustering Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/clustering-artifactory.md): Artifactory's clustered Active/Active architecture provides a full set of true high availability features and is supported with an Artifactory
- [HA Architecture](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/ha-architecture.md): JFrog Platform Deployment high-availability architecture is built on three blocks: a load balancer, multiple application nodes, and shared storage.
- [High Availability](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/high-availability.md): JFrog Platform services support High Availability clusters of two or more nodes on the same LAN, requiring an Enterprise X or Enterprise+ license.
- [System Architecture](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/system-architecture.md): The JFrog Platform Deployment (JPD) is a microservices-based architecture where each product — Artifactory, Xray, Distribution — runs with its own services and a local Router for API gateway and service discovery.
- [Artifactory Federation Service (RTFS)](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-federation-service-rtfs.md): The JFrog Artifactory Federation Service (RTFS) enables federated repositories across multiple JPD sites, maintaining a single source of truth for binaries across geographically distributed deployments.
- [Evidence Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/evidence-service.md): Artifactory enables you to attach evidence (signed metadata) to a subject, designated such as an artifact, build, package, or Release Bundle v2.
- [JFrog Artifactory Product](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-artifactory-product.md): JFrog Artifactory is composed of multiple microservices — including the Artifactory Service, Router, Access, and Metadata — each with a distinct role in the platform.
- [Individual JVM for Access Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/individual-jvm-for-access-service.md): JFrog is implementing a significant architectural update to Artifactory to improve resource management and strengthen the infrastructure. Starting from Artifactory version 7.
- [IPv6 Support](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/ipv6-support.md): Starting from version 6.0.0, Artifactory supports IPv6-enabled hosts. IPv6 support allows users to configure an IPv6 _context.url_ in the _ha-node.
- [JFConnect Microservice](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfconnect-microservice.md): JFConnect manages entitlements for the JFrog Platform Deployment, enabling dynamic license allocation based on account changes in JFrog's entitlements server.
- [JFrog Distribution Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-distribution-service.md): The Distribution service contains Distribution and Router Microservices.
- [JFrog Products Container Base Image](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-products-container-base-image.md): Artifactory uses the Red Hat UBI Micro container base image for lightweight, secure container deployments.
- [JFrog Router Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-router-service.md): JFrog Router is a microservice that handles the registration of the other microservices within the JFrog Platform.
- [JFrog Xray Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-xray-service.md)
- [Application Directory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/application-directory.md): The JFrog Platform application directory contains immutable product binaries, scripts, and default configurations. It is separate from the var directory, which holds mutable runtime data.
- [Data Directory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/data-directory.md): The data directory (mutable) contains all customizable configurations, by data generated the application, and logs. The environment variable is JFROG_HOME/<product>/var.
- [Generic Directory Layout](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/generic-directory-layout.md): All JFrog products share a common directory structure under $JFROG_HOME, with standardized subdirectories for app binaries, configuration, data, and logs.
- [System Directories](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/system-directories.md): JFrog products have a common directory structure for each installation The type. following sections describe the directory structures in detail:
- [JFrog Home](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-home.md): JFrog Home is the default home directory of the JFrog Platform that the is contains application directory and the data directory. The environment variable JFROG_HOME.
- [Configure Proxy Between JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-proxy-between-jfrog-products.md): Configure an HTTP proxy between JFrog Platform products in corporate environments where all cross-node communication is routed through a corporate proxy server.
- [Create a Private-Public Key Pair](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-a-private-public-key-pair.md): Create a private-public key pair using OpenSSL for use with JFrog Platform token certificate configuration. The key pair is used for signing and validating access tokens.
- [Custom Token Certificates](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/custom-token-certificates.md): Configure custom token certificates for the JFrog Platform by providing your own CA and token issuer key pair, replacing the auto-generated certificates used for signing access tokens.
- [Load the Key Pair to the JFrog Platform Deployment (JPD) through Bootstrap Files](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/load-the-key-pair-to-the-jfrog-platform-deployment-jpd-through-bootstrap-files.md): During certificate initialization, certificates and private keys are read from the following paths, if present: Load the key pair to the JFrog Platform Deployment (JPD) through bootstrap files
- [Communication Between Services](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/communication-between-services.md): The JFrog Platform is composed of multiple services for each JFrog running product, on multiple nodes. Communication between services is done through router services.
- [Create an automatic admin token](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-an-automatic-admin-token.md): _From Artifactory release 7.38.4._ JFrog enables companies to create their own admin-scoped access token without using the JFrog Platform UI or via another token.
- [Manage Keys](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/manage-keys.md): Manage master keys, join keys, and pairing tokens for JFrog Platform deployments. Covers key rotation, best practices, and establishing secure communication between JFrog services.
- [Pairing Tokens](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/pairing-tokens.md): Available from Artifactory version 7.29.7, a pairing token establishes trust between different JFrog microservices.
- [Using Access as a Certificate Authority](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/using-access-as-a-certificate-authority.md): Use the JFrog Access service as a Certificate Authority (CA) to issue TLS certificates for inter-service communication within the JFrog Platform.
- [Set TLS on the JFrog Platform](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/set-tls-on-the-jfrog-platform.md): Enable TLS on the JFrog Platform using Access as a certificate authority to enforce encrypted inter-service communication across all JPD components.
- [Download TLS Certificates](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/download-tls-certificates.md): Download TLS certificates from an SSL-secured JFrog Platform server using OpenSSL commands for use in client certificate configurations.
- [Using TLS Certificates as a Client](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/using-tls-certificates-as-a-client.md): Configure the JFrog Platform to trust external TLS certificates so it can establish secure connections to LDAPS, remote repositories, and other external services.
- [Trust a self-signed certificate or a new CA](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/trust-a-self-signed-certificate-or-a-new-ca.md): To trust a new certificate, add the certificate to the directory of every $JFROG_HOME/<product>/var/etc/security/keys/trusted service that needs to trust it.
- [Artifactory Configuration Descriptors](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-configuration-descriptors.md): Artifactory configuration descriptors are deprecated; refer to the Configuration Files page for current information on JFrog Artifactory configuration files.
- [Artifactory Configuration XSD](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-configuration-xsd.md): All Artifactory configuration files can be found under the folder. Unlike $JFROG_HOME/artifactory/var/etc/artifactory the default artifactory.config.
- [Artifactory Security](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-security.md): Artifactory provides several system-wide settings to control access to In different resources. the Administration module, go to Artifactory > Security. Some remote repositories (e.
- [Backups](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/backups.md): Artifactory enables you to configure the automatic backup of your system settings, then provide access to that backup in the case you need to restore it.
- [General Settings](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/general-settings.md): With the Artifactory General Settings, you can configure various global in parameters Artifactory. To configure the Artifactory General Settings:
- [HTTP Settings](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/http-settings.md): In many cases, an organization may provide access to Artifactory through a reverse proxy such as NGINX or Apache.
- [Import and Export](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/import-and-export.md): JFrog Artifactory supports import and export at two levels: individual repositories and the full system, including configuration, security, and stored data.
- [Configuring Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configuring-artifactory.md): JFrog Artifactory general configuration settings are managed in the Administration module under Artifactory > General > Settings.
- [Maven Indexer](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/maven-indexer.md): Artifactory exposes Maven indexes for use by Maven integrations of common IDEs ( for example, IntelliJ IDEA, NetBeans, Eclipse).
- [RSA Key Pairs](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/rsa-key-pairs.md): JFrog Artifactory lets you store and manage multiple RSA public and private key pairs used to sign and verify Alpine Linux index files.
- [WebStart and Jar Signing](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/webstart-and-jar-signing.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to sign JAR files and support Java Web Start (JNLP) deployments, enabling clients to download and run signed Java applications directly from Artifactory.
- [Choose the right database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/choose-the-right-database.md): The JFrog Platform is a mission-critical tool for building and delivering software.
- [Database Connection Settings](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-connection-settings.md): Configure database connection pool settings for JFrog Artifactory in system.yaml, including connection limits, timeout values, and driver-specific parameters.
- [Database setup workflow](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-setup-workflow.md): Set up a database for JFrog Artifactory by creating an instance, creating a dedicated user, installing a JDBC driver, and configuring system.yaml.
- [Database tuning for heavy loads in Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-tuning-for-heavy-loads-in-artifactory.md): Tune JFrog Artifactory database performance under heavy loads by adjusting maxOpenConnections in system.yaml to control the connection pool across all nodes.
- [Database Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-configuration.md): Configure an external database for JFrog Artifactory. Supported databases include PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL, MariaDB, and Microsoft SQL Server, with PostgreSQL recommended for all new deployments.
- [Configure Artifactory to use MariaDB](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-mariadb.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to connect to an external MariaDB database by downloading the JDBC driver and updating the database settings in system.yaml.
- [Create the Artifactory MariaDB Database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-the-artifactory-mariadb-database.md): Artifactory provides a script that will execute the SQL commands you need to create your MariaDB database.
- [Increase MariaDB Default Packet Size](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/increase-mariadb-default-packet-size.md): Increase the MariaDB maximum allowed packet size for JFrog Artifactory to prevent errors when storing large build and configuration data in the database.
- [MariaDB for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/mariadb-for-artifactory.md): JFrog Artifactory supports MariaDB as a database backend; configure a dedicated MariaDB instance and update Artifactory settings to use it.
- [Known Issues with MariaDB](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/known-issues-with-mariadb.md): Known issues and limitations when using MariaDB with JFrog Artifactory, including JDBC driver compatibility.
- [Tune MariaDB for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/tune-mariadb-for-artifactory.md): Optimize MariaDB performance for JFrog Artifactory by enabling the InnoDB engine and applying recommended tuning parameters in /etc/my.cnf.
- [Configure Artifactory to use Microsoft SQL Server](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-microsoft-sql-server.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to connect to an external Microsoft SQL Server database by installing the JDBC driver and updating the system.yaml database settings.
- [Create the Artifactory Microsoft SQL Server Database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-the-artifactory-microsoft-sql-server-database.md): Create the Microsoft SQL Server database and user required for JFrog Artifactory, and grant the necessary permissions before starting Artifactory.
- [Microsoft SQL Server for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/microsoft-sql-server-for-artifactory.md): Use Microsoft SQL Server as the external database for JFrog Artifactory to leverage enterprise SQL Server features including backup, restore, and high availability.
- [Configure Artifactory to use MySQL](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-mysql.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to connect to an external MySQL database by downloading the JDBC driver and updating the database connection settings in system.yaml.
- [Create the Artifactory MySQL Database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-the-artifactory-mysql-database.md): Artifactory provides a script that will execute the SQL commands you need to create your MySQL database.
- [Increase MySQL Default Packet Size](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/increase-mysql-default-packet-size.md): Increase the MySQL maximum allowed packet size for JFrog Artifactory to prevent errors when storing large build and configuration data in the database.
- [MySQL for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/mysql-for-artifactory.md): Use MySQL as the external database for JFrog Artifactory to leverage MySQL infrastructure features such as backup, restore, and replication.
- [Tune MySQL for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/tune-mysql-for-artifactory.md): Optimize MySQL performance for JFrog Artifactory by enabling the InnoDB engine and applying recommended tuning parameters in /etc/my.cnf.
- [Configure Artifactory to use Oracle](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-oracle.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to connect to an external Oracle database by installing the JDBC driver, creating the schema, and updating system.yaml.
- [Create the Artifactory Oracle Database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-the-artifactory-oracle-database.md): Create and configure an Oracle database for JFrog Artifactory, including tablespace setup, user creation, and BLOB storage configuration.
- [Oracle for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/oracle-for-artifactory.md): Use Oracle Database as the external database for JFrog Artifactory to leverage Oracle infrastructure features such as backup, restore, and high availability.
- [Configure Artifactory HA to Use PostgreSQL Database in HA in Ansible](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-ha-to-use-postgresql-database-in-ha.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory HA in Ansible to use external PostgreSQL instances deployed in high-availability mode for increased resiliency.
- [Configure Artifactory to Use Advisory Locks in PostgreSQL](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-advisory-locks-in-postgresql.md): JFrog Artifactory 7.77.3 and later supports PostgreSQL advisory locks as an alternative to distributed table locks, reducing contention in high-throughput environments.
- [Configure Artifactory to Use PostgreSQL Single Node in Ansible](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-postgresql-single-node.md): Configure a single-node JFrog Artifactory instance in Ansible to use a dedicated PostgreSQL database by editing the system.yaml connection settings.
- [Configure Artifactory to Use PostgreSQL](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-postgresql.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to store artifact metadata in PostgreSQL while keeping binary data on the filesystem under $JFROG_HOME.
- [Create the Artifactory PostgreSQL Database](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/create-the-artifactory-postgresql-database.md): Use the commands below to create an Artifactory user and database with the appropriate permissions. Modify relevant values to match your specific environment.
- [Enable PostgreSQL Connectivity from Remote Servers](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/enable-postgresql-connectivity-from-remote-servers.md): Enable remote server access to the PostgreSQL database used by JFrog Artifactory by configuring pg_hba.conf and reloading the PostgreSQL service.
- [Enable TLS Encryption for PostgreSQL](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/enable-tls-encryption-for-postgresql.md): To enable Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption for PostgreSQL, set to the sslmode property verify-full in the JDBC connector URL
- [PostgreSQL for Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/postgresql-for-artifactory.md): JFrog Artifactory supports PostgreSQL as its database, simplifying infrastructure by aligning with the database used by all other JFrog products.
- [Amazon S3 Template Parameters](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/amazon-s3-template-parameters.md): Reference for all parameters supported by the Amazon S3 Official SDK binary provider template for configuring JFrog Artifactory filestore on AWS S3.
- [Configure Artifactory to Use S3 Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-artifactory-to-use-s3-storage.md): Artifactory uses the standard S3 SDK for authentication. Users should use one of the following methods for authentication: 1.
- [AWS S3 Object Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/aws-s3-object-storage.md)
- [Integrate Artifactory with AWS SDK v2 for S3 Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/integrate-artifactory-with-aws-sdk-v2-for-s3-storage.md): Artifactory's S3 binary storage provider supports AWS SDK v2 starting from Artifactory 7.125.4.
- [Migrate Your Filestore to S3](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrate-your-filestore-to-s3.md): Migrate your JFrog Artifactory filestore to Amazon S3 object storage using either the manual migration method or the filestore migration tool.
- [S3 Binary Storage Templates](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/s3-binary-storage-templates.md): Configure the S3 binary storage provider for JFrog Artifactory using the available S3 templates, setting account-specific parameters while keeping recommended defaults.
- [Using the S3 Intelligent Tiering Storage Class](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/using-the-s3-intelligent-tiering-storage-class.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory to use the Amazon S3 Intelligent Tiering storage class, which automatically moves artifacts between access tiers to reduce storage costs.
- [Azure Blob Storage Cluster V1 Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-blob-storage-cluster-v1-binary-provider.md): Configure the Azure Blob Storage Cluster V1 binary provider for JFrog Artifactory HA deployments, using the sharding-cluster provider with Azure Blob as the remote filestore.
- [Azure Blob Storage Cluster V2 Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-blob-storage-cluster-v2-binary-provider.md): Configure the Azure Blob Storage Cluster V2 binary provider for JFrog Artifactory HA deployments, using the updated sharding-cluster provider with Azure Blob as the remote filestore.
- [Azure Blob Storage V1 Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-blob-storage-v1-binary-provider.md): Configure the azure-blob-storage V1 binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to use Azure Blob Storage as the remote filestore for single-node deployments.
- [Azure Blob Storage V2 Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-blob-storage-v2-binary-provider.md): Configure the azure-blob-storage-v2 binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to use Azure Blob Storage as the remote filestore with the updated V2 provider implementation.
- [Azure Workload Identity](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-workload-identity.md): JFrog Artifactory supports Azure Workload Identity for secret-less authentication to Azure Blob Storage on AKS using federated identity credentials.
- [Azure Blob Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/azure-blob-storage.md): Artifactory supports managing your Artifactory filestore on the cloud with Azure Blob Storage
- [Manual Filestore Migration for Azure Blob Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/manual-filestore-migration-for-azure-blob-storage.md): To migrate your filestore manually, you need to execute the following 1. steps: Stop Artifactory.
- [Set up Artifactory to Use Azure Blob Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/set-up-artifactory-to-use-azure-blob-storage.md): Artifactory requires the use of Azure block blob storage, a storage system optimized for unstructured text and binary data.
- [Cached Filesystem Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/cached-filesystem-binary-provider.md): Configure the cache-fs binary provider for JFrog Artifactory, which acts as a local LRU cache layer in front of a remote filestore to improve artifact access performance.
- [Direct Cloud Storage Download](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/direct-cloud-storage-download.md): Direct Cloud Storage Download lets JFrog Artifactory redirect artifact download requests straight to cloud storage, reducing load on Artifactory nodes.
- [Step 1: Configure the Artifactory Filestore for Direct Cloud Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/step-1-configure-the-artifactory-filestore-for-direct-cloud-storage.md): As described in Filestore Configuration, you control how Artifactory using stores binaries the$JFROG_HOME/artifactory/var/etc/artifactory/binarystore.xmlfile.
- [Step 2: Specify Repositories for Direct Download](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/step-2-specify-repositories-for-direct-download.md): Specify which JFrog Artifactory repositories use direct cloud storage download by enabling the redirect option per repository — there is no global redirect setting.
- [Step 3: Finetune Direct Download](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/step-3-finetune-direct-download.md): Fine-tune direct cloud storage download settings in self-hosted JFrog Artifactory to control redirect thresholds, signed URL expiry, and caching behavior.
- [Step 4: Control Your Signed URL Downloads](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/step-4-control-your-signed-url-downloads.md): Control signed URL redirect behavior in self-hosted JFrog Artifactory using the X-JFrog-Download-Redirect-To header to specify the target storage provider.
- [Step 5: Configure Firewalls](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/step-5-configure-firewalls.md): When direct cloud storage downloads are enabled, it means that artifacts requested will actually be served from the S3 or CloudFront domains rather than from Artifactory's domain.
- [Eventual Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/eventual-binary-provider.md): Configure the eventual binary provider for JFrog Artifactory, used as part of a provider chain for remote filestores that exhibit upload latency or eventual consistency.
- [Eventual-Cluster Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/eventual-cluster-binary-provider.md): Configure the eventual-cluster binary provider for JFrog Artifactory HA deployments, used in provider chains for remote filestores with upload latency in clustered environments.
- [File-System Binary Provider Template Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/file-system-binary-provider-template-configuration.md): Configure the file-system binary provider template for JFrog Artifactory by updating binarystore.xml with the local filesystem storage path and optional cache settings.
- [File System Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/file-system-binary-provider.md): This is the basic filestore configuration for Artifactory and is used for a local or mounted filestore.
- [Configure a Sharding Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-a-sharding-binary-provider.md): Configure a sharding binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to distribute artifact storage across multiple physical mounts or cloud storage buckets for increased capacity and redundancy.
- [Configure Sharding for HA Cluster](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-sharding-for-ha-cluster.md): For a high availability cluster, Artifactory offers templates that file supportsharding-clusterfor system, S3, and Google Storage.
- [Double Shards Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/double-shards-binary-provider.md): Configure the double-shards binary provider template for JFrog Artifactory, which shards artifact storage across two physical mounts with one copy per shard for capacity expansion.
- [Filestore Sharding](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/filestore-sharding.md): Configure filestore sharding for JFrog Artifactory to distribute artifact storage across multiple mounts or cloud buckets, enabling horizontal scaling of binary storage.
- [Redundant Shards Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/redundant-shards-binary-provider.md): Configure the redundant-shards binary provider for JFrog Artifactory, which stores two copies across two physical mounts to provide redundancy alongside sharded storage capacity.
- [Restoring Balance in Unbalanced Redundant Storage Units](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/restoring-balance-in-unbalanced-redundant-storage-units.md): Restore balance in an unbalanced JFrog Artifactory redundant sharding filestore after voluntary operations such as filestore migration that cause shard imbalance.
- [Sharding-Cluster Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/sharding-cluster-binary-provider.md): Configure the sharding-cluster binary provider for JFrog Artifactory HA deployments to combine local and cloud-native storage in a sharded cluster configuration.
- [State-Aware Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/state-aware-binary-provider.md): Configure the state-aware binary provider for JFrog Artifactory sharding deployments, which monitors the health of underlying storage mounts and reroutes writes during failures.
- [Using Balancing to Recover from Mount Failure](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/using-balancing-to-recover-from-mount-failure.md): Recover from a mount failure in a JFrog Artifactory sharded filestore using balancing to redistribute artifacts and restore full redundancy after a storage unit is repaired.
- [Full-DB and Full-DB-Direct Binary Providers](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/full-db-and-full-db-direct-binary-providers.md): Configure the full-db or full-db-direct binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to store all artifact metadata and binary content as blobs directly in the database.
- [Google Storage Binary Provider Native Client Template](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/google-storage-binary-provider-native-client-template.md): Configure the google-storage-v2 binary provider template for JFrog Artifactory to use Google Cloud Storage as the remote filestore via the Google native client.
- [Google Storage V2 Cluster Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/google-storage-v2-cluster-binary-provider.md): Configure the Google Storage V2 Cluster binary provider for JFrog Artifactory HA deployments using the Google native client with sharding for clustered filestore.
- [Google Cloud Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/google-cloud-storage.md): Artifactory fully supports Google Cloud Storage (GCS) so your Artifactory filestore can reside on the cloud.
- [Migrate Your Filestore to Google Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrate-your-filestore-to-google-storage.md): To migrate your filestore, you need to execute the following steps. 1. Stop Artifactory.
- [Set up Artifactory to use GCS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/set-up-artifactory-to-use-gcs.md): In order to move your Artifactory filestore to the cloud, you need to the execute following steps: 1. Shut down Artifactory.
- [Filestore Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/filestore-configuration-landing.md)
- [Remote Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/remote-binary-provider.md): Configure the remote binary provider for JFrog Artifactory as part of a multi-provider chain connecting a local cache to a remote filestore backend.
- [Retry Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/retry-binary-provider.md): Configure the retry binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to automatically retry failed binary operations in provider chains that include unreliable remote filestores.
- [State-Aware Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/state-aware-binary-provider_filestore-configuration.md): Configure the state-aware binary provider for JFrog Artifactory to monitor storage health and reroute artifact writes when an underlying sharding or sharding-cluster mount is unhealthy.
- [State-Aware-S3 Binary Provider](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/state-aware-s3-binary-provider.md): Configure the state-aware-s3 binary provider for JFrog Artifactory S3 sharding deployments to monitor S3 bucket health and reroute writes during bucket failures.
- [Configure JFrog Platform for Passwordless Access to EKS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-jfrog-platform-for-passwordless-access-to-eks.md): After you configure the passwordless access in the EKS platform, you must configure the JFrog Platform with the information. 1.
- [Configure the EKS Cluster with the AWS policy and IAM role](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-the-eks-cluster-with-the-aws-policy-and-iam-role.md): Configure an Amazon EKS cluster with the required AWS IAM policy and role to enable passwordless access for JFrog Platform services running in Kubernetes.
- [Passwordless Access for Amazon EKS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/passwordless-access-for-amazon-eks.md): JFrog Platform can leverage AWS AssumeRole for passwordless access to Amazon EKS clusters without storing credentials.
- [Install the JFrog Registry Operator in EKS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-the-jfrog-registry-operator-in-eks.md): Install the JFrog Registry Operator in an Amazon EKS cluster to enable passwordless authentication between Kubernetes workloads and JFrog Artifactory.
- [Permissions for Passwordless Access to EKS Cluster](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/permissions-for-passwordless-access-to-eks-cluster.md): The Helm charts for the JFrog Registry operator create the following in permissions the EKS cluster before you continue with the configuration process.
- [Access Bootstrap YAML File](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/access-bootstrap-yaml-file.md): Bootstrap a JFrog Platform environment quickly by providing an Access bootstrap YAML file to pre-configure access provider authentication settings.
- [Access YAML Configuration Files](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/access-yaml-configuration-files.md): JFrog Platform Access service configuration files reside under $JFROG_HOME/artifactory/var/etc/access and control authentication, tokens, and signing keys.
- [Enable New Integrations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/enable-new-integrations.md): To enable creating a new integration with the JFrog Platform, you need to set the flag for new integrations in the access.config.latest.yaml file. 1. Copy the access.config.latest.
- [Access YAML Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/access-yaml-configuration.md): Access provides a flexible means of configuration using a simple YAML file configuration found in the $JFROG_HOME/artifactory/var/etc/access folder.
- [Migrated Authentication Provider Properties](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrated-authentication-provider-properties.md): As part of enhancements to the JFrog Access Service to make it the primary service for authentication providers, from Artifactory version 7.71.
- [Revert to Previous Access Configurations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/revert-to-previous-access-configurations.md): Every time you load a new access.config.import.yml file, Access backs up a the current configuration in files named access.config.latest.<timestamp>.yml.
- [Supported Access Configurations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/supported-access-configurations.md): Reference for all supported configuration parameters in the JFrog Access YAML file, including security settings, token policies, and service communication options.
- [Upload the Access YAML File](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upload-the-access-yaml-file.md): Upload a custom access.yaml configuration file to JFrog Access to apply security and token settings without restarting the JFrog Platform services.
- [Artifactory Bootstrap YAML File](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-bootstrap-yaml-file.md): Setting up Artifactory using the Bootstrap YAML configuration file is a to convenient alternative going through the startup wizard.
- [Artifactory System Properties](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-system-properties.md): Instead of configuring properties in the JVM runtime configuration of the hosting container, you can edit the artifactory.system.properites file
- [Artifactory Configuration Files](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-configuration-files.md): JFrog Artifactory configuration files are located under $JFROG_HOME/artifactory/var/etc/artifactory, with each file controlling a specific aspect of the service.
- [Advanced Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/advanced-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Advanced configuration options for Artifactory YAML including maintenance, garbage collection, and system-level settings.
- [Apply Artifactory YAML Configuration Changes](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/apply-artifactory-yaml-configuration-changes.md): Apply JFrog Artifactory YAML configuration changes by sending a PATCH request to the Artifactory REST API endpoint after updating the artifactory.config.import.xml file.
- [Artifactory YAML File Format](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-yaml-file-format.md): Reference for the JFrog Artifactory YAML configuration file format, including key-value structure, entity syntax, and rules for constructing valid configuration entries.
- [Delete an existing element in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/delete-an-existing-element-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Delete existing configuration elements from JFrog Artifactory using the YAML configuration file by sending a PATCH request with a null value for the target field.
- [General (General, Property Sets, Proxy, Mail) Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/general-general-property-sets-proxy-mail-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): General, property set, proxy, and mail server configurations in the YAML Artifactory configuration file.
- [Artifactory YAML Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-yaml-configuration.md): Artifactory provides a flexible way to configure an existing Artifactory a instance using simple YAML configuration file.
- [Replication (push and pull) Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/replication-push-and-pull-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Push and pull replication configuration settings in the Artifactory YAML configuration file.
- [Repositories Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/repositories-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Local, remote, and virtual repository configuration settings in the YAML Artifactory configuration file.
- [Security Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/security-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Security configuration settings including authentication, access control, and encryption in the Artifactory YAML configuration file.
- [Service Configurations in Artifactory YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/service-configurations-in-artifactory-yaml.md): Service configuration settings including backups, indexing, and garbage in collection the Artifactory YAML configuration file.
- [Distribution Application Config YAML File](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-application-config-yaml-file.md): The YAML configuration file offers an alternative way to specify your for initial settings JFrog Distribution.
- [System Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/system-configuration.md): JFrog Platform configuration relies on YAML files installed with the system; this page explains the different configuration file types, their purposes, and their locations.
- [Artifactory System YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-system-yaml.md): A complete example of the JFrog Artifactory system.yaml file showing all configurable parameters for database, filestore, security, and service settings.
- [Distribution System YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-system-yaml.md): The Distribution system YAML file contains all the parameters that can be configured. The YAML file is constructed with keys and entities, using the following
- [Implementing Configuration as Code in JFrog](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/implementing-configuration-as-code-in-jfrog.md): JFrog provides a flexible way to configure your system using a single file system.yaml configuration found in the $JFROG_HOME/<product>/var/etc folder for each product.
- [System YAML Configuration File](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/system-yaml-configuration-file.md): The JFrog Platform system.yaml file is the central configuration file for all self-managed JFrog products, enabling configuration-as-code for shared and per-service settings.
- [Using System YAMLs](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/using-system-yamls.md): Reference for the JFrog Platform system.yaml file format, including key-entity structure, syntax rules, and how to apply configuration changes across JFrog services.
- [Xray System YAML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-system-yaml.md): A complete example of the JFrog Xray system.yaml file showing all configurable parameters for database, RabbitMQ, storage, and server settings.
- [Worker YAML Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/worker-yaml-configuration.md): The Worker System YAML supports shared configurations, Worker microservice configurations, and router configurations.
- [Activate AI ML](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/activate-ai-ml.md): JFrog AI/ML integrates everything Machine Learning and data science teams need to build and scale ML models in production, yielding a unified AI solution for every need.
- [Install the Artifactory Federation Service](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-artifactory-federation-service.md): The Artifactory Federation Service (RTFS) is used for the timely of huge synchronization volumes of artifact metadata between customer sites.
- [Installing Mission Control](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-mission-control.md): Enable the JFrog Mission Control microservice on self-hosted JFrog Artifactory installations using Docker, Docker Compose, RPM, Debian, Linux, or Helm.
- [Get Started with the Self-Managed Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/getting-started.md): Install the JFrog Platform on Kubernetes (including OpenShift) using Helm. Choose an evaluation quick start or production-grade deployment path for Artifactory, Xray, and Distribution.
- [Choose Your Installation Path](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/choose-your-installation-path.md): Use this decision tree to choose between Helm chart (Kubernetes, including OpenShift) and Ansible (VMs/bare-metal) for your JFrog Platform deployment — production or evaluation.
- [New To JFrog](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/new-to-jfrog.md): New to JFrog? Learn platform architecture, run a Helm-based evaluation install, and plan your production deployment — step by step.
- [Why Platform Helm Chart?](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/why-platform-helm-chart.md): Compare JFrog Platform installation methods — Helm chart vs Ansible vs standalone installers. Learn why Helm is recommended for Kubernetes and OpenShift deployments.
- [Installation & Configuration](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installation-configuration.md): Install and configure the JFrog Platform self-managed on Linux, Windows, Docker, and Kubernetes (Helm). Covers architecture, databases, filestore, and advanced Helm options.
- [Installing the JFrog Platform](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-the-jfrog-platform.md): Install the full JFrog Platform using the recommended Helm chart on Kubernetes or Ansible for VMs. Includes Artifactory, Xray, Distribution, and all services in one deployment.
- [Install JFrog Platform via Ansible Collection [Quick Start]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-platform-via-ansible-quick-start.md): Deploy the JFrog Platform — Artifactory, Xray, and Distribution — on Linux VMs using the jfrog.platform Ansible collection.
- [Install Individual JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install.md): Learn how to install JFrog products with detailed guides and recommended practices.
- [Bridge Client Docker Compose Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/bridge-client-docker-compose-installation.md): Install the JFrog Bridge Client service on a self-hosted environment using Docker Compose by downloading the installer from JFrog releases and running the setup script.
- [Bridge Client Helm Chart Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/bridge-client-helm-chart-installation.md): Install the JFrog Bridge Client service on a Kubernetes environment using a Helm chart by adding the JFrog Helm repository and running a helm install command.
- [Bridge Client Linux Archive Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/bridge-client-linux-archive-installation.md): Install the JFrog Bridge Client service on Linux using the Linux archive method. Review prerequisites and verify system requirements before beginning the installation.

- [Docker](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/docker.md): Install and configure JFrog Artifactory using Docker and Docker Compose with comprehensive setup instructions for both single-node and high-availability configurations.

- [Distribution HA Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-ha-helm-installation.md): Install JFrog Distribution in high-availability mode on Kubernetes using the official Helm chart, with an external PostgreSQL database and shared filestore.
- [Distribution HA Linux Archive/RPM/Debian Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-ha-linux-archive-rpm-debian-installation.md): Install JFrog Distribution in high-availability mode using Linux Archive, RPM, or Debian packages with step-by-step multi-node configuration instructions.

- [Distribution Single Node Docker Compose Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-single-node-docker-compose-installation.md): Install JFrog Distribution as a single node using Docker Compose by downloading the installer, configuring system.yaml, and starting the service.
- [Distribution Single Node Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-single-node-helm-installation.md): Install JFrog Distribution as a single node on Kubernetes using Helm by adding the JFrog Helm repository and deploying with a custom values file.
- [Distribution Single Node Interactive Script Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-single-node-interactive-script-installation.md): The installer script provides you an interactive way to install and its Distribution dependencies. All install types are supported.
- [Distribution Single Node Linux Archive Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-single-node-linux-archive-installation.md): Install the following prerequisites independently of the Distribution Make archive installation. sure to install them before starting your Xray service.
- [Distribution Single Node RPM Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-single-node-rpm-installation.md): The RPM installation bundles Distribution and all its dependencies. It is provided as native RPM packages, where Distribution and its dependencies must be installed separately.
- [Installing Distribution](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-distribution.md): Install JFrog Distribution in single-node or high-availability mode using Docker Compose, Helm, RPM, Debian, or Linux archive on an Enterprise+ license.
- [Installing JFrog Advanced Security](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-jfrog-advanced-security.md): JFrog Advanced Security (JAS) requires Xray version 3.67.x and later for Helm, Xray version 3.73.5 and later for RPM, Debian, and Linux Archive, and Xray version 3.73.
- [Install JFrog Advanced Security on your Self-Hosted Environment with Helm](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-advanced-security-on-your-self-hosted-environment-with-helm.md): Install JFrog Advanced Security on a self-hosted environment using Helm, either into an existing JFrog Platform or alongside a new installation.
- [Install JFrog Advanced Security on your Self-Hosted Environment without Helm](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-advanced-security-on-your-self-hosted-environment-without-helm.md): Install JFrog Advanced Security on a self-hosted environment without Helm using a streamlined K3s-based Kubernetes setup for users without an existing cluster.
- [JFrog Advanced Security Additional Configurations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-advanced-security-additional-configurations.md): Additional configuration options for JFrog Advanced Security in self-hosted deployments, including tuning default values for cloud and on-premises environments.
- [JFrog Advanced Security Prerequisites](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-advanced-security-prerequisites.md): Review the system requirements and prerequisites for JFrog Advanced Security before installation, including supported platforms, hardware minimums, and license requirements.
- [JFrog Advanced Security Readiness Checking](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/jfrog-advanced-security-readiness-checking.md): Validate that your self-hosted JFrog Platform is correctly configured and ready to use JFrog Advanced Security by running through these readiness checks.
- [Installing JFrog Runtime Security](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-jfrog-runtime-security.md): Install JFrog Runtime Security to monitor containerized workloads at runtime. Includes CPU and memory sizing guidelines for Runtime Integrity nodes averaging 100 monitored pods.
- [Installing Workers](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-workers.md): Workers is a JFrog Platform service that enables you to control your execution flows.
- [Install JFrog Worker with Artifactory Helm Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-worker-with-artifactory-helm-chart.md): Install JFrog Worker on a self-hosted environment using the JFrog Artifactory and Worker Helm charts to enable custom event-driven automation scripts.
- [Install JFrog Worker with Docker Compose](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-worker-with-docker-compose.md): Install JFrog Worker on a self-hosted environment using Docker Compose to deploy the Worker service alongside JFrog Artifactory.
- [Install JFrog Worker with Platform Helm Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-worker-with-platform-helm-chart.md): Install JFrog Worker on a self-hosted environment using the JFrog Platform Helm chart by enabling the Worker service in your custom values file.
- [Installing Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/installing-xray.md): Install and configure JFrog Xray in single-node or high-availability mode. Xray 3.x requires JFrog Artifactory 7.x and supports PostgreSQL as the external database.
- [Install Xray Single Node with Interactive Script](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-xray-single-node-with-interactive-script.md): The installer script works with all supported upgrade methods (Linux RPM, Archive, Debian and Docker Compose).
- [Install Xray Single Node with Linux Archive](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-xray-single-node-with-linux-archive.md): Install the following prerequisites independently of the Xray archive Make installation. sure to install them before starting your Xray service.
- [Install Xray with FIPS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-xray-with-fips.md): Install JFrog Xray with FIPS 140-2 compliance enabled on RPM-based Linux systems using the Linux archive installer with FIPS mode activated at the OS level.
- [PostgreSQL for Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/postgresql-for-xray.md): JFrog Xray requires PostgreSQL as its database; configure a dedicated PostgreSQL instance and point Xray to it using system.yaml settings.
- [Quorum Queue Enablement for Xray and Platform Helm Charts](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/quorum-queue-enablement-for-xray-and-platform-helm-charts.md): Support for quorum queues has been added in Xray chart version 3.124.x. Currently, Xray uses RabbitMQ with mirrored classic queues.
- [RabbitMQ (Split) for Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/rabbitmq-split-for-xray.md): This is a guide for installing Xray and RabbitMQ on separate nodes and to connecting multiple Xray nodes a RabbitMQ cluster (sequentially) to achieve high availability.
- [Xray HA Docker Compose Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-ha-docker-compose-installation.md): Install JFrog Xray in high-availability mode using Docker Compose by deploying multiple Xray nodes sharing an external PostgreSQL database and RabbitMQ message broker.
- [Xray HA Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-ha-helm-installation.md): Install JFrog Xray in high-availability mode on Kubernetes using Helm by configuring a multi-node cluster with shared PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ services.
- [Xray HA Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-ha-installation.md): Set up a JFrog Xray high-availability cluster with two or more nodes using Helm, Docker Compose, or Linux archive installation methods.
- [Xray HA Linux Archive / RPM/ Debian Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-ha-linux-archive-rpm-debian-installation.md): Install JFrog Xray in high-availability mode on Linux using the archive, RPM, or Debian package, adding additional nodes after completing the single-node installation.
- [Xray HA OpenShift Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-ha-openshift-installation.md): Xray on OpenShift is available from version 3.80.9 onwards. Before you the proceed with installation, review the system requirements.
- [Xray Single Node Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-helm-installation.md): Complete guide for installing Xray Single Node using Helm charts with step-by-step instructions and configuration options.
- [Xray Single Node Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-installation.md): Install JFrog Xray on a single node using RPM, Debian, Linux archive, or Docker Compose. Xray requires a running JFrog Artifactory instance and PostgreSQL database.
- [Xray Single Node Manual Debian Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-manual-debian-installation.md): Install JFrog Xray as a single node on Debian manually by downloading the package, configuring system.yaml, and starting the Xray service.
- [Xray Single Node Manual Docker Compose Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-manual-docker-compose-installation.md): Manually install JFrog Xray on a single node using Docker Compose, configuring the compose file directly. Review system requirements before proceeding.
- [Xray Single Node Manual RPM Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-manual-rpm-installation.md): The RPM installation bundles Xray and all its dependencies. It is provided as native RPM packages, where Xray and its dependencies must be installed separately.
- [Xray Single Node OpenShift Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-single-node-openshift-installation.md): Xray on OpenShift is available from version 3.80.9 onwards. Before you the proceed with installation, review the system requirements.
- [Verifying Signatures of JFrog Binaries](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/verifying-signatures-of-jfrog-binaries.md): Starting from January 26, 2025, JFrog has replaced its primary GPG (GNU be Privacy Guard) key, and all the JFrog binaries will signed with a new 4096-bit RSA primary GPG key.
- [Avoid Repositories from Migration [OPTIONAL]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/avoid-repositories-from-migration-optional.md): After you run the migrator tool with the getconfig option, the migrator tool creates the repositories.yaml file inside the folder, <working directory>/migration/nexus-migrator.
- [Avoid Security Entities from Migration [OPTIONAL]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/avoid-security-entities-from-migration-optional.md): After you run the migrator tool with the getconfig option, the migrator tool creates the security.json file inside the folder, <working directory>/migration/nexus-migrator.
- [Before you begin](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/before-you-begin-1.md): Before beginning migration, verify that the following system requirements are in place. A warning appears in Nexus Repository Manager after you enable script execution.
- [Edit Migration Configuration [OPTIONAL]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/edit-migration-configuration-optional.md): After you run the migrator tool with the config option, the migrator tool creates the migrationConfig.yaml file inside the folder, <working directory>/migration/nexus-migrator.
- [Edit Repository Names [OPTIONAL]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/edit-repository-names-optional.md): After you run the migrator tool with the getconfig option, the migrator tool creates the repositories.list file inside the folder, <working directory>/migration/nexus-migrator.
- [Edit Security Entities [OPTIONAL]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/edit-security-entities-optional.md): After you run the migrator tool with the getconfig option, the migrator tool creates the securityRegexMap.listfile inside the folder, <working directory>/migration/nexus-migrator.
- [Migrating from Sonatype Nexus Repository Manager to Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrating-from-sonatype-nexus-repository-manager-to-artifactory.md): Migrate from Sonatype Nexus Repository Manager to JFrog Artifactory using the Nexus migrator tool to transfer repositories, artifacts, and security configuration.
- [Migration Process](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migration-process.md): Run the Nexus-to-JFrog Artifactory migration script in a single stage or as separate sequential stages to migrate repositories, artifacts, and security settings.
- [Migrator Tool Overview](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrator-tool-overview.md): The migrator tool uses Docker, installed on the Nexus Repository Manager machine, to facilitate the migration.
- [Migration of Privileges](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migration-of-privileges.md): Roles and Privileges in Nexus Repository Manager are mapped to Groups and Permission Targets respectively in Artifactory. In Nexus 3, the following privileges will be migrated:
- [Repositories and Users in Nexus Repository Manager and Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/repositories-and-users-in-nexus-repository-manager-and-artifactory.md): When there is an existing repository with the same name as in Artifactory, the migration of artifacts will fail if the repository is of a different type than the Nexus Repository.
- [Nexus Migrator Release Notes](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/nexus-migrator-release-notes.md): Release notes for the JFrog Nexus Migrator tool, listing fixes, improvements, and new features across all published versions.
- [Repository Migration Status](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/repository-migration-status.md): Monitor the status of repository migration from Sonatype Nexus to JFrog Artifactory using the repositories.yaml file maintained by the Nexus migrator tool.
- [Run the Migration Tool in a Single Step](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/run-the-migration-tool-in-a-single-step.md): Migrate all Nexus entities to JFrog Artifactory in a single step by running the migration script with the "all" argument and providing Artifactory connection details.
- [Run the Migration Tool in Multiple Stages](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/run-the-migration-tool-in-multiple-stages.md): Run the JFrog Nexus migrator tool in multiple stages to migrate artifacts from Sonatype Nexus to Artifactory incrementally, executing config, security, and artifact stages separately.
