- [Uninstall Artifactory Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/uninstall-artifactory-helm-installation.md): Remove a JFrog Artifactory Helm release from your cluster while preserving persistent volume claims, using Helm v3.17 or later.
- [Uninstall JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/uninstalling-jfrog-products.md): Uninstall JFrog Platform products — including Artifactory, Xray, and Distribution — across Linux, Docker, Docker Compose, RPM, and Debian installations.
- [Uninstalling JFrog Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/uninstalling-jfrog-artifactory.md): Remove JFrog Artifactory from your server by following the uninstall steps that match your original installation method (Linux, Docker, Helm, RPM, or Debian).
- [Uninstalling JFrog Distribution](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/uninstalling-jfrog-distribution.md): Go to the directory where you extracted the installer and delete that 1. directory. Stop Mission Control and all the third party services.
- [Uninstalling JFrog Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/uninstalling-jfrog-xray.md): Go to the directory where you extracted the installer and delete that 1. directory. Stop Xray and all third party services associated with it.
- [Upgrade JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade.md): Upgrade JFrog Platform products including Artifactory, Xray, and Distribution using the same installation method as the original deployment.
- [Upgrading Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-artifactory.md): Upgrade JFrog Artifactory using the same method as your original installation — Helm, Docker, RPM, Debian, Linux Archive, or Windows Archive.
- [Upgrade Docker](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade-docker.md): Upgrading Artifactory using Docker provides a reliable, containerized to approach managing your instance. This method simplifies the upgrade process and its dependencies.
- [Upgrade Helm Charts](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade-helm-charts.md): Upgrade JFrog Artifactory on Kubernetes using Helm charts, which provide a declarative and repeatable method to manage application version updates with a single command.
- [Upgrade Linux Package](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade-linux-package.md): Upgrading Artifactory on Linux using native packages provides a process. straightforward, production-ready
- [Upgrade Windows Archive](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade-windows-archive.md): Upgrading Artifactory on Windows is a straightforward process that the new involves downloading .
- [Upgrading from Artifactory Version 6.10.x Onwards to 7.x](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-from-artifactory-version-6-10-x-onwards-to-7-x.md): Upgrade JFrog Artifactory from version 6.10.x or later to 7.x using the supported upgrade path. Review the version compatibility table to determine if an interim upgrade step is required.
- [Upgrading Distribution](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-distribution.md): The procedure to upgrade Distribution depends on your installation type. We strongly recommend reading through this page before proceeding with your upgrade.
- [Upgrading from Distribution Version 2.x to 2.x](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-from-distribution-version-2-x-to-2-x.md): Upgrade JFrog Distribution from one 2.x version to another using Helm, Docker Compose, RPM, Debian, or Linux Archive with the JFrog installer script.
- [Upgrading JFrog Catalog](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-jfrog-catalog.md): Docker Compose Starting from Xray Version 3.131.x Valkey has been added as a cache service in Catalog.
- [Upgrading Mission Control](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-mission-control.md): JFrog Mission Control upgrades automatically as part of JFrog Artifactory updates, since it is integrated as an Artifactory microservice from release 7.27 onward.
- [Upgrading Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-xray.md): The procedure to upgrade Xray depends on your installation type. We this strongly recommend reading through page before proceeding with your upgrade.
- [Migrating to Quorum Queue or Quorum Queue Enablement for Xray in Native installer](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/migrating-to-quorum-queue-or-quorum-queue-enablement-for-xray-in-native-installer.md): JFrog has added support for RabbitMQ Quorum Queues, available as an in has optional parameter system.yaml, because RabbitMQ deprecated Classic Queue mirroring in version 4.x.
- [Upgrade Xray with FIPS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrade-xray-with-fips.md): Upgrade JFrog Xray in FIPS-compliant environments by stopping the service, applying the new package, and restarting with FIPS mode enabled.
- [Upgrading from Xray Version 2.7 to 3.x](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-from-xray-version-2-7-to-3-x.md): JFrog Xray v3.x is only compatible with JFrog Artifactory v7.x. To you The upgrade, must first install JFrog Artifactory 7.x. following upgrade methods are supported:
- [Upgrading from Xray Version 3.x to 3.x](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-from-xray-version-3-x-to-3-x.md): Upgrade JFrog Xray from one 3.x version to another using Helm, Docker Compose, RPM, Debian, or Linux Archive with the JFrog installer script.
- [Upgrading to RabbitMQ 4 in bundled RabbitMQ-Xray[Docker]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-to-rabbitmq-4-in-bundled-rabbitmq-xray-docker.md): To upgrade to RabbitMQ 4, update the docker-compose-rabbitmq.yaml with 1. “4.1.1-management” image tag. Stop Xray and RabbitMQ
- [Upgrading to RabbitMQ 4 in bundled RabbitMQ-Xray [RPM/DEB/Linux Archive]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/upgrading-to-rabbitmq-4-in-bundled-rabbitmq-xray-rpm-deb-linux-archive.md): To upgrade to RabbitMQ 4, set the JF_PRODUCT_RABBITMQ_HOME variable key to ${JF_PRODUCT_HOME}/app/third-party/rabbitmq4 present in ${JF_PRODUCT_HOME}/app/bin/xray.
- [Advanced Options for Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/advanced-options-for-xray.md): Advanced Helm configuration options for JFrog Xray, including RabbitMQ tuning, custom certificates, resource limits, and external secret management.
- [Disable TLS in RabbitMQ for Xray in JFrog Platform Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/disable-tls-in-rabbitmq-for-xray-in-jfrog-platform-chart.md): To disable TLS in RabbitMQ, set global.rabbitmq.auth.tls.enabled and false rabbitmq.auth.tls.enabledas in the values.yaml file and run the following command.
- [Enable TLS in RabbitMQ for Xray in JFrog Platform Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/enable-tls-in-rabbitmq-for-xray-in-jfrog-platform-chart.md): Available from JFrog Platform Chart version 10.14.1. 1. Set and true in global.rabbitmq.auth.tls.enabled rabbitmq.auth.tls.enabledas the values.yaml file.
- [Enable TLS in RabbitMQ in Xray Helm Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/enable-tls-in-rabbitmq-in-xray-helm-chart.md): Available from Xray version 3.78.10. 1. Set rabbitmq.auth.tls.enabled as true in the values.yaml file.
- [Advanced Options for Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/advanced-options-for-xray.md): Configure advanced JFrog Xray options for Helm deployments, including custom certificates, RabbitMQ settings, split messaging topology, and external database configuration.
- [All Products - Customization](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/all-products-customization.md): Advanced Helm customizations applicable to all JFrog products, including TLS configuration, JFConnect proxy, custom volumes, sidecars, and init containers.
- [Add Circle of Trust Certificates](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-circle-of-trust-certificates.md): Add Circle of Trust certificates to JFrog Platform Helm deployments by creating a Kubernetes secret with the root certificate from the source machine and mounting it into the product pods.
- [Add Custom Init Containers in Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-custom-init-containers-in-helm-installation.md): Add custom init containers to JFrog Platform Helm deployments to run preparatory tasks such as configuration injection or secret fetching before the main product container starts.
- [Add Custom Sidecar Containers in Helm Installations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-custom-sidecars-containers-in-helm-installations.md): Add custom sidecar containers to JFrog Platform Helm deployments to run helper services such as log shippers or monitoring agents alongside the main product containers.
- [Add Custom Volumes in Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-custom-volumes-in-helm-installation.md): A Kubernetes volume is essentially a directory that is accessible to all a the containers running in pod.
- [Auto-generated Passwords (Internal PostgreSQL)](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/auto-generated-passwords-internal-postgresql.md): An internal PostgreSQL requires one password variable to be available or during installation upgrade.
- [Establish TLS in Artifactory and JFrog Platform](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/establish-tls-in-artifactory-and-jfrog-platform.md): In HTTPS, communication is encrypted using Transport Layer Security (TLS). By default, TLS between JFrog Platform nodes is disabled.
- [All Products - Customization](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/all-products-customization.md): Advanced Helm customizations applicable to all JFrog products, including TLS configuration, JFConnect proxy, custom volumes, sidecars, and init containers.
- [Override the Default System YAML File in Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/override-the-default-system-yaml-file-in-helm-installation.md): Override the default system.yaml configuration for JFrog Platform products in Helm deployments by mounting a custom ConfigMap or secret into the pod.
- [Use Custom Secrets in Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-custom-secrets-in-helm-installation.md): Secrets are Kubernetes objects that are used for storing sensitive data as such username and passwords with encryption.
- [Use Unified Secret in Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-unified-secret-in-helm-installation.md): Use a unified Kubernetes secret in JFrog Platform Helm deployments to consolidate all product secrets into a single secret object, simplifying secret management in production clusters.
- [Artifactory Advanced Options](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-advanced-options.md): Advanced Helm configuration options for JFrog Artifactory, including custom persistence, resource limits, JVM tuning, and integration with external secrets managers.
- [Add Extensions](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-extensions.md): Add plugins and extensions to JFrog Artifactory in Helm deployments to extend Artifactory's functionality with custom integrations and workflows.
- [Add Licenses Using Secrets](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-licenses-using-secrets.md): Add JFrog Artifactory license keys to a Helm deployment using Kubernetes secrets, either by referencing an existing secret or creating one during the Helm release.
- [Add Memory Target Trigger to Artifactory Charts using HPA](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-memory-target-trigger-to-artifactory-charts-using-hpa.md): Starting from Artifactory version 7.111, for the Artifactory chart, HPA is will function only when the replica count a minimum of 2 (i.e., in High Availability mode).
- [Advanced Storage Options](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/advanced-storage-options.md): Configure advanced filestore options for JFrog Artifactory Helm deployments, including persistent volumes, external object storage, and custom binary store settings.
- [Bootstraps Artifactory](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/bootstraps-artifactory.md): Bootstrap JFrog Artifactory admin credentials and initial configuration automatically during a Helm chart deployment using predefined values.
- [Configure readOnlyRootFilesystem in Artifactory Containers](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-readonlyrootfilesystem-in-artifactory-containers.md): Starting from Artifactory version 7.111, you can leverage the Kubernetes security context feature in Artifactory containers.
- [Artifactory Advanced Options](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-advanced-options.md): Advanced Helm customizations specific to JFrog Artifactory, including license injection, admin bootstrapping, storage options, SSL termination, and security settings.
- [Infrastructure Customization](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/infrastructure-customization.md): Artifactory Memory and CPU Resources The Artifactory Helm chart comes with support for configured resource requests and limits to Artifactory, Nginx and PostgreSQL.
- [Install Artifactory and Artifactory HA with Nginx and Terminate SSL in Nginx Service (Load Balancer)](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-artifactory-and-artifactory-ha-with-nginx-and-terminate-ssl-in-nginx-service-load-balancer.md): Install JFrog Artifactory or Artifactory HA with Nginx via Helm and terminate SSL at the LoadBalancer layer using certificates such as AWS ACM.
- [Log Analytics](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/log-analytics.md): FluentD, Prometheus and Grafana To configure Prometheus and Grafana to the gather metrics from Artifactory through use of FluentD, refer to the
- [Monitoring and Logging in Artifactory Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/monitoring-and-logging-in-artifactory-helm-installation.md): Artifactory exposes MBeans under the org.jfrog.artifactory domain, which enables you to monitor repositories, executor pools, storage and HTTP connection pools.
- [Run Ingress Behind Another Load Balancer](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/run-ingress-behind-another-load-balancer.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory Helm deployments to run Nginx Ingress behind an external load balancer that handles TLS offloading or sets X-Forwarded-* headers.
- [Security-related Issues](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/security-related-issues.md): Address security concerns in JFrog Artifactory Helm deployments, including database password customization, Kubernetes secrets management, and network policy configuration.
- [Use an External Database with Artifactory Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-an-external-database-with-artifactory-helm-installation.md): For production grade installations, it is recommended to use an external a PostgreSQL with static password.
- [Use ConfigMaps to Store Non-confidential Data](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-configmaps-to-store-non-confidential-data.md): Use Kubernetes ConfigMaps to store and inject non-confidential configuration data into JFrog Artifactory Helm deployments as environment variables or mounted files.
- [Artifactory High Availability](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-high-availability.md): Advanced Helm customizations specific to JFrog Artifactory high-availability deployments, including persistent storage, node affinity, and shared filestore configuration.
- [Add Licenses with Artifactory HA Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/add-licenses-with-artifactory-ha-helm-installation.md): To activate Artifactory HA, you must install an appropriate license as of part the installation. There are three ways to manage the licenses:
- [Artifactory Storage](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-storage.md): Artifactory HA support a wide range of storage back ends (for more see information, Filestore Configuration).
- [Artifactory High Availability](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-high-availability.md): Advanced Helm customizations specific to JFrog Artifactory high-availability deployments, including persistent storage, node affinity, and shared filestore configuration.
- [Scale the Artifactory HA Helm Installation Cluster](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/scale-the-artifactory-ha-helm-installation-cluster.md): Ensure that all pods from the Artifactory HA chart serve as primary. Set this flag: --set artifactory.primary.replicaCount=${CLUSTER_SIZE}
- [Use an Existing Shared Volume Claim with Artifactory HA Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-an-existing-shared-volume-claim-with-artifactory-ha-helm-installation.md): Configure JFrog Artifactory HA Helm deployments to use an existing shared PersistentVolumeClaim for data and backup storage shared across all Artifactory nodes.
- [Use an Existing Volume Claim for Artifactory HA Helm Installation](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/use-an-existing-volume-claim-for-artifactory-ha-helm-installation.md): To use an existing volume claim for the Artifactory primary node storage: 1. - [Distribution Custom Volumes](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-custom-volumes.md): Add custom volumes to JFrog Distribution Helm deployments by defining volume entries in values.yaml for use with init containers or custom file system content.
- [Helm Charts for Advanced Users](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/helm-charts-for-advanced-users.md): Use of Helm Charts for JFrog installation provides a wide range of in to advanced functionalities addition the basic installers.
- [Install the JFrog Platform Using Ansible](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-the-jfrog-platform-using-ansible.md): Advanced configuration for the JFrog Platform Ansible collection: external database, high availability, TLS, NGINX, Ansible Vault, and upgrades.
- [Install the JFrog Platform using Helm Chart](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-the-jfrog-platform-using-helm-chart.md): Post-install configuration for the JFrog Platform Helm chart: license installation, enabling products, product configuration, and external PostgreSQL.
- [Load Balancer](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/load-balancer.md): Configure a load balancer as the entry point for a JFrog Platform high-availability deployment to distribute incoming requests optimally across Artifactory server nodes.
- [JFrog Platform Onboarding Wizard](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/onboarding-wizard.md): The JFrog Platform onboarding wizard guides you through initial setup — admin password, license activation, base URL, proxy, and repository configuration — on first login.
- [Configure JFrog Platform in an Air-gapped Environment](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/configure-jfrog-platform-in-an-air-gapped-environment.md): An air-gapped environment is a system without access to the outside Internet.
- [Allowlisted URLs and Air-Gapped Environment Considerations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/allowlisted-urls-and-air-gapped-environment-considerations.md): For seamless operations and connectivity with JFrog servers, we recommend that your organization allowlist the following URLs in your proxy settings or firewall outbound rules.
- [Artifactory Filestore](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-filestore.md): The filestore is where the binaries are physically stored. Artifactory the provides following options to store binaries:
- [Artifactory System Requirements and Platform Support](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): System requirements, supported platforms, and third-party dependencies for JFrog Artifactory self-managed deployments.
- [Artifactory Database Requirements](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-database-requirements.md): Supported databases and version requirements for JFrog Artifactory, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, MariaDB, and Microsoft SQL Server with HA notes.
- [Database and Third-Party Applications in Distribution](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-and-third-party-applications-in-distribution_distribution-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Distribution supports the following versions of PostgreSQL: Distribution HA requires an external database.
- [Database and Third-Party Applications in Xray](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-and-third-party-applications-in-xray_xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Comprehensive guide to database and third-party application requirements for JFrog Xray, including PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Erlang version compatibility.
- [Database Support Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/database-support-matrix.md): Supported databases by JFrog product: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MS SQL Server, Oracle, and Derby.
- [Distribution System Requirements and Platform Support](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): System requirements, supported platforms, and third-party dependencies for JFrog Distribution self-managed deployments.
- [Docker Requirements](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/docker-requirements_general-system-requirements.md): For Docker and Docker Compose installations, JFrog services require Docker Engine 25.0 and above, and Docker Compose v2.
- [Helm Chart Requirements](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/helm-chart-requirements_general-system-requirements.md): Review prerequisites for deploying JFrog products with Helm charts, including supported Kubernetes and OpenShift versions, Helm version, and resource requirements.
- [General System Requirements](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/general-system-requirements.md): General system requirements for running JFrog Platform products, covering hardware, filestore, database, Docker, Helm, and OS prerequisites.
- [Java Requirements for JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/java-requirements-for-jfrog-products.md): Artifactory and Distribution are Java-based product that require JDK 21 or higher to run. OpenJDK is already bundled into the applications.
- [Preflight Check](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/preflight-check.md): The diagnosticUtil Utility is used to check if your VM or bare metal meets server the minimum requirements before installing JFrog products.
- [Supported Browsers](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/supported-browsers.md): Artifactory has been tested with the latest versions of the following browsers:
- [System Requirements](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/system-requirements.md): Explore the system requirements for the JFrog Platform and its products, including Artifactory, Xray, and Distribution.
- [Artifactory Network Ports](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/artifactory-network-ports.md): Artifactory, Xray, and other JFrog products require static IP addresses and direct network communication across required ports.
- [Distribution Network Ports](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/distribution-network-ports_distribution-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Distribution uses port 8082 by default for external communication and additional internal ports for JFrog Platform microservice communication.
- [Network Requirements for JFrog Products](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/network-requirements-for-jfrog-products.md): Artifactory, Xray, and other JFrog products all need to be set with static IP addresses.
- [Xray Network Ports](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-network-ports_xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Xray uses port 8082 by default for external communication and additional internal ports for JFrog Platform microservice communication.
- [Feature Comparison Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/feature-comparison-matrix-for-self-mangaged-jpds.md): Compare components and services included in each JFrog Platform edition, from commercial subscriptions to free non-commercial licenses like OSS and JCR.
- [Hardware Sizing Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/hardware-sizing-matrix.md): Artifactory system requirements depend mainly upon the expected number of active clients. Use this page to understand sizing, steps, and reference links.
- [Requirements Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/requirements-matrix.md): Platform support, hardware sizing, software dependencies, product dependencies, and license eligibility matrices for JFrog self-managed deployments.
- [Product License Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/license-matrix-pro-x-enterprise-x-enterprise.md): Use this matrix to confirm which JFrog Platform products and services are included in Pro X, Enterprise X, and Enterprise license tiers before selecting your deployment scope.
- [Product Dependencies Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/product-dependencies-matrix.md): Use this matrix to validate product-to-product and infrastructure dependencies for JFrog Platform products before installation, ensuring all required services are in place.
- [Software Dependencies Matrix](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/software-dependencies-matrix.md): Minimum and recommended versions for core software dependencies used by JFrog self-managed deployments.
- [Supported Platforms And OS](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/supported-platforms-and-os.md): Operating system and platform support matrix for JFrog self-managed products, covering x86-64 and ARM64 architectures, Kubernetes, and OpenShift versions.
- [Xray System Requirements and Platform Support](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Learn about Xray's system requirements, sizing configurations, and platform support for different deployment scenarios.
- [RabbitMQ HA in Xray using Quorum Queues](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/rabbitmq-ha-in-xray-using-quorum-queues.md): RabbitMQ is a critical component of the Xray architecture, serving as a to message bus facilitate communication between various application services.
- [Xray and RabbitMQ Nodes Recommendations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-and-rabbitmq-nodes-recommendations_xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): To mitigate performance bottlenecks, avoid port conflicts, and prevent use unusual configurations, a dedicated node for Xray and RabbitMQ with no other software running. Xray
- [Xray File Handle Allocation Limit](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-file-handle-allocation-limit_xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): Check and increase the file handle allocation limit on Linux systems running JFrog Xray to meet the minimum required open file handles for production deployments.
- [Xray Storage Recommendations](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/xray-storage-recommendations_xray-system-requirements-and-platform-support.md): JFrog Xray performs best with SSD storage; NFS is not recommended due to I/O bottlenecks, and local storage does not require replication in HA deployments.
- [Install JFrog Platform via Helm [Quick Start]](https://docs.jfrog.com/installation/docs/install-jfrog-platform-via-helm-quick-start.md): Deploy the entire JFrog Platform — Artifactory, Xray, Distribution, Catalog, and more — from a single Helm chart.

- [Unshare Repositories Across Projects](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/docs/unshare-repositories-across-projects.md)
- [View Projects in a Dashboard View](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/docs/view-projects-in-a-dashboard-view.md)
- [Project Creation Tasks](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/docs/project-creation-tasks.md)
- [Project Management Tasks](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/docs/project-management-tasks.md)
- [Project User Management Tasks](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/docs/project-user-management-tasks.md)

## API Reference
- [Add or Update User in Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/addorupdateprojectuser.md): Adds or updates a user in a project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role, or a project admin with the Manage Members admin privilege enabled.
- [Move Repository in a Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/attachrepositorytoproject.md): If not assigned, assigns a repository to a project. If already assigned, moves the project repository to the specified location, and unassigns the repository from the previous project.  Note: When you assign a resource to a project, the project owns and manages the resource. When you share a resource with a project, the project does not own the resource.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Create Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/createproject.md): Create a new Project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.  For more information about creating tokens, see Create Token.
- [Create Role](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/createprojectrole.md): Adds a new role to the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Delete Existing Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/deleteproject.md): Deletes an existing project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Delete Existing Project's Group](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/deleteprojectgroup.md): Deletes the existing project group.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user assigned the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Delete an Existing Role](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/deleteprojectrole.md): Deletes an existing project role.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Delete Existing Project's User](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/deleteprojectuser.md): Deletes an existing project's user.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Get Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getproject.md): Returns the desired project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Project Admin role or a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Get Projects Group](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectgroup.md): Returns the requested group associated with the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Get Projects Groups](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectgroups.md): Returns all groups associated with the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Get Status of Project Repository](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectrepositorystatus.md): Returns the status of a repository and whether it was assigned and/or shared to a project, to multiple projects, or to all projects.  Note: When you assign a resource to a project, the project owns and manages the resource. When you share a resource with a project, the project does not own the resource.  Since: 7.90.0  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Get Project Role](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectrole.md): Returns the requested project role.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Project Admin role or a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Get Roles List](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectroles.md): Returns all project roles.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Get Projects List for a Global Role](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectsforrole.md): Returns a list of projects where a specific global role is used.  Since: 7.117.1  Security: Requires admin permissions.
- [Get Projects List](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectslist.md): Returns all projects the requesting user manages (Project Admin or Platform Administrator).  Since: 7.117.4  Security: Requires authentication using Access Tokens, either as admin, a user with the Platform Administrator role, or starting from Artifactory Self-Hosted version 7.117.5, using a scoped token with the `project:<project_key>:r` scope. Note that if the request is issued by a Project Admin, only the projects listed under the Project Admin's scope will be listed.
- [Get Project User](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectuser.md): Returns the requested user associated with the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Get Project Users](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/getprojectusers.md): Returns all users associated with the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires authentication using Access Tokens, either as admin, a user with the Platform Administrator role, or starting from Artifactory Self-Hosted version 7.117.5, using a scoped token with the `project:<project_key>/members/**:r` scope. Note that if the request is issued by a Project Admin, only the projects listed under the Project Admin's scope will be listed.
- [Share Repository with All Projects](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/sharerepositorywithallprojects.md): Shares a local or remote repository across all the Projects allowing members in more than one Project to have access to the repository. Project Members are granted actions to the shared repository according to their Roles and Role actions assigned in the target Project.  Note: When you assign a resource to a project, the project owns and manages the resource. When you share a resource with a project, the project does not own the resource.  Since: 7.15.3  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Share Repository with Target Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/sharerepositorywithtargetproject.md): Shares a local or remote repository with a specific target Project. Project Members of the target project are granted actions to the shared repository according to their Roles and Role actions assigned in the target Project.  Note: When you assign a resource to a project, the project owns and manages the resource. When you share a resource with a project, the project does not own the resource.  Since: 7.15.3  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Unassign a Project from a Repository](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/unassignprojectfromrepository.md): Unassigns a project from a repository.  Note: You can assign a Project to a repository using the Move Repository command.  Since: 7.17.1  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Unshare Repository with All Projects](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/unsharerepositorywithallprojects.md): Unshares a shared local or remote repository. The repository is not deleted but remains assigned to the source repository.  Since: 7.15.3  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Unshare Repository with Target Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/unsharerepositorywithtargetproject.md): Unshares a local or remote repository with a specific target Project.  Since: 7.15.3  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Update Existing Project Properties](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/updateproject.md): Updates an existing project's properties.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role.
- [Update Group in Project](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/updateprojectgroup.md): Updates the group in the project.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.
- [Update an Existing Project Role](https://docs.jfrog.com/projects/reference/updateprojectrole.md): Updates an existing project role.  Since: 7.17.4  Security: Requires a user with the Platform Administrator role or Project Admin permissions if admin_privileges.manage_resources is enabled.


# User Management Documentation

## Guides
- [Getting Started with JFrog User Management](https://docs.jfrog.com/user-management/docs/getting-started.md): Manage your personal identity, security credentials, and account settings within the JFrog Platform.
- [API Key](https://docs.jfrog.com/user-management/docs/api-key.md)
- [Identity Tokens](https://docs.jfrog.com/user-management/docs/identity-tokens.md)
- [Manage User Profile](https://docs.jfrog.com/user-management/docs/manage-user-profile.md)
- [User Profile](https://docs.jfrog.com/user-management/docs/user-profile.md)
